seed_problem: Place watchtowers on a forest graph to cover every cell within radius r, minimizing total cost; score = baseline_cost / achieved_cost.
mutation: composite: re-skin as smart-city adaptive lighting and generalize the seed R-dominating set to a WEIGHTED minimum-cost R-MULTI-cover -- each vertex u carries an illumination demand d_u and must be reached by at least d_u distinct lamps within R hops (demands > 1), turning uniform single-cover into set-multicover where cost-ratio greedy, coverage-greedy and redundancy pruning genuinely diverge; the build-everywhere construction is the calibrated baseline B.
title: Smart-City Adaptive Lighting: Minimum-Cost Multi-Coverage Lamp Placement
strategies: build a lamp on every intersection (reproduces the checker baseline B, ratio = 0.1)
coverage-greedy: repeatedly build the lamp that satisfies the most outstanding demand units (ignores cost)
cost-ratio greedy: repeatedly build the lamp maximizing newly-satisfied-demand / build-cost
cost-ratio greedy followed by redundancy pruning (drop the most expensive lamps whose removal keeps every demand met)
LP-rounding of the fractional multi-cover relaxation, or local-search add/drop swaps
